mnemonics: Metric Spaces Diameter: M - Maximum distance between any two points in a set.
S - Supremum of all pairwise distances.
D - Defined only for bounded sets.
keyPoints: The diameter of a set A in a metric space (X, d) is sup{d(x, y) : x, y ∈ A}.
A set is bounded if and only if its diameter is finite.
The diameter is zero if and only if the set has at most one point.
commonMistakes: Confusing diameter with radius: diameter is the maximum distance between any two points, while radius is often used in the context of balls and is half the diameter in Euclidean spaces.
Forgetting that the diameter is defined only if the set is bounded; unbounded sets have an infinite diameter.
Miscalculating the diameter by not considering all pairs of points, especially in non-Euclidean metrics.
